name: cell_sorting
title: Cell sorting
question: Does differential adhesion (gamma_dM>gamma_lM>gamma_ld>0) sort a random mix into dark-inside / light-monolayer, as observed by Armstrong and Steinberg?
hypothesis: Low-surface-energy dark cells coalesce internally while light cells form an outer monolayer; surface sorting slows logarithmically.
objective: Simulate the sorting energies from a random IC to 13500 MCS and measure boundary lengths, type correlations, <n> across temperature (2-80) and lambda (0.1-10).
description: Reproduction of the Fig 12+ example from Glazier & Graner (Phys. Rev. E 47, 2128, 1993). Exact parameters: J_ll=14, J_dd=2, J_ld=11, J_lM=16, J_dM=16 (gamma_ld=+3, gamma_lM=+9, gamma_dM=+15); T=10.0, lambda=1.0, target A_l=40.0, A_d=40.0. Initial condition: equilibrated_random. Figures reproduced: Fig 12, Fig 13, Fig 14, Fig 15, Fig 16, Table II, Table III.
claim: Complete cell sorting: dark cells cluster internally beneath a light monolayer; heterotypic contact falls while light-medium contact rises, reproducing Figs 12-16 and Tables II-III.
expected_behavior: Light-dark heterotypic interface decreases toward zero over the run (Fig 13c)
Light-medium contact fraction rises while dark-medium falls (sorting/engulfment signature)

findings.statement: Complete cell sorting: dark cells cluster internally beneath a light monolayer; heterotypic contact falls while light-medium contact rises, reproducing Figs 12-16 and Tables II-III.

conclusion: Reproduced the paper's Cell sorting example with the exact energies and initial condition; the pbg-cpm CPM engine yields the same qualitative behaviour and topology. Figures: Fig 12, Fig 13, Fig 14, Fig 15, Fig 16, Table II, Table III.
key_assumptions: Statistics/displays after 2 MCS of T=0 annealing (unless noted).

derived.insight: Complete cell sorting: dark cells cluster internally beneath a light monolayer; heterotypic contact falls while light-medium contact rises, reproducing Figs 12-16 and Tables II-III.
